CS361 Artificial Life and Digital Evolution S25 Schedule

Syllabus FAQ

This is a rough schedule of topics for each day and the release and due dates for each assignment.

Class topics and homework information may change over the course of the term. Please treat this schedule as a rough guide for seeing what we’ll be focusing on, and expect that there will be shifts as the term progresses.

The slide links are all restricted to Carleton folks, so make sure you’re logged in to your Carleton account if you have one. If you are outside of Carleton and would like to see them, just shoot me an email!

Date Assignment/Reading Due Class Topic Assignment Out
Week 1      
M 3/31 What is AL Prep What is (artificial) life? Slides  
W 4/2 C++ Prep Getting Started and C++, Slides  
F 4/4 Evolutionary Algorithms Evolutionary Algorithm Lab, Slides  
Week 2      
M 4/7 Read about CA project, Week 2 Checkin Finishing EA Lab, Slides  
W 4/9 Cellular Automata Prep Finding Neighbors, Slides CA project
F 4/11 CA 2 Prep Gradient Lab, Slides  
Week 3      
M 4/14 Inheritance Prep Inheritance in C++, CA Peer Review, Slides  
W 4/16 Ecosystem Models Prep, CA project Empirical World Intro, Slides AE project
F 4/18 Artificial Ecologies Continuing Empirical Intro Lab, Slides  
Week 4      
M 4/21 Avida Prep Peer review of AE projects; Avida-Ed, Slides  
W 4/23 Data Collection Prep Empirical Data Lab, Slides Encyclopedia assignment
F 4/25 AE project AE project help time, Slides  
Week 5      
M 4/28 Digital Evolution Prep Signal-GP Lite Lab, Slides DE project
W 4/30 Encyclopedia topic, Configuration Preparation Using Empirical Configuration, Slides  
F 5/2 Check-in questions DE Project help time, Slides  
Week 6      
M 5/5   Mid-term Break!  
W 5/7 DE project, group preference form Project introduction;Peer review of DE projects, Slides Project proposal
F 5/9 Guest Speaker Prep Guest Speaker: Dr. Alexander Lalejini, Slides  
Week 7      
M 5/12 Project topic checkin; Encyclopedia first drafts; Git Prep Peer review of encyclopedia drafts; Git Lab, Slides  
W 5/14 Guest Speaker Prep Guest Speaker: Dr. Luis Zaman, Slides  
F 5/16 Attend 5/15 Bits and Bytes if you are able Visiting Speaker: Dr. Betty Cheng, Slides  
Week 8      
M 5/19 Project proposal Proposal peer review, Slides Final project
W 5/21 Encyclopedia second drafts Graphing Data, Slides  
F 5/23   Work time, Slides  
Week 9      
M 5/26 Guest Speaker Prep Guest Speaker: Dr. Matthew A. Moreno, Slides  
W 5/28 Replication reports Sharing reports, Slides  
F 5/30 Guest Speaker Prep Guest Speaker: Dr. Emily Dolson, Slides  
Week 10      
M 6/2 Guest Speaker Prep Guest Speaker: Dr. Lisa Soros, Slides  
W 6/4 Encyclopedia final draft due 10pm Final project showcase; course evaluations, Slides  
Exam Period      
  Nothing scheduled, projects due 6/9 8:30pm (+1 hour grace period)    
    Any request for an extension must go through the Dean of Students office as I am not otherwise permitted to consider work submitted after this time.

  • anyaevostinar
  • AnyaEVostinar

The homepage of Prof. Anya E. Vostinar. I teach computer science and research the evolution of symbiosis using digital evolution and agent-based modeling. I wrote the Symbulation platform.
